Phase-41.NAME: KPprotoN fake-TLS production data-plane rollback
Phase-41.Goal: Replace the Phase-40 official MTProxy production path with the KPprotoN fake-TLS SNI data-plane while keeping KrotPN as the registration, assignment, dashboard, admin, and backup control-plane.

OperatorProblem.item-1: Clean VPS deploy starts, email registration works, and users receive MTProto links, but Telegram Desktop/mobile still reports unavailable or unstable proxy behavior.
OperatorProblem.item-2: Phase-40 official MTProxy requires active-secret manifest changes and process restart when a new user secret is added, which does not match the product requirement for per-user always-on proxy issuance.
OperatorProblem.item-3: The reference project CodexKPprotoN_for_KPN and the Habr architecture describe the intended model: per-user fake-TLS SNI policy under a wildcard domain, not official dd-secret manifest sync.

ArchitectureDecision.item-1: Phase-41 supersedes M-052/M-053 as the production MTProto path and returns the live data-plane to KPprotoN fake-TLS with per-SNI policy.
ArchitectureDecision.item-2: KrotPN remains the source of truth for verified users, assignments, owner payloads, admin revoke/reissue, and backup/restore metadata.
ArchitectureDecision.item-3: New owner payloads use server=u-*.krotpn.xyz, port=443, and a KPprotoN-compatible ee fake-TLS secret derived from assignment SNI, base secret, and private salt.
ArchitectureDecision.item-4: RU public TCP 443 uses SNI routing: web SNI goes to nginx fallback, issued user SNI goes to DE KPprotoN runtime, and unknown SNI takes an explicit safe fallback.
ArchitectureDecision.item-5: DE KPprotoN runtime accepts policy apply/revoke/replay over the private token-protected policy API without restarting the data-plane for each new user.

NonGoals.item-1: No redesign of install.sh or the operator-approved deploy workflow beyond the narrow KPprotoN runtime wiring needed to replace official MTProxy.
NonGoals.item-2: No Lets Encrypt automation change; the operator-provided wildcard TLS certificate model remains in force.
NonGoals.item-3: No billing, trial, VPN, Resend, or referral behavior changes.
NonGoals.item-4: No public exposure of policy API, runtime token, base secret, salt, derived secret, full tg link, wildcard private key, or provider secrets.
NonGoals.item-5: No claim of MTProto readiness from container health, policy_count, or fake-TLS accept alone; Telegram Desktop/mobile proof remains required.

DataFlow.flow-1: Verified email activation creates or reuses one canonical MTProto assignment in KrotPN Postgres.
DataFlow.flow-2: M-043 derives the assignment SNI and fake-TLS owner secret and returns an owner-only tg://proxy or https://t.me/proxy payload.
DataFlow.flow-3: M-044 applies the issued SNI to the DE KPprotoN policy API before the user sees active proxy status.
DataFlow.flow-4: M-050 routes u-*.krotpn.xyz traffic from RU public 443 to the DE KPprotoN runtime while krotpn.xyz and www.krotpn.xyz continue to the web fallback.
DataFlow.flow-5: M-049 serves fake-TLS Telegram MTProto traffic on DE and uses bounded Telegram bootstrap/static fallback behavior aligned with the reference KPprotoN implementation.
DataFlow.flow-6: M-051 proves the issued user path with redacted diagnostics and operator Telegram Desktop/mobile evidence.

Steps.step-1: Mark Phase-40 official MTProxy modules M-052/M-053 as superseded for production and keep them as rollback/reference only.
Steps.step-2: Restore M-043 provisioning to KPprotoN fake-TLS payloads with server=u-*.krotpn.xyz and secret=ee...; official dd assignments must be reissued explicitly.
Steps.step-3: Restore M-044 policy apply/revoke/replay as the production runtime bridge and remove official secret-manifest sync from active issuance.
Steps.step-4: Reconcile M-049 mtproto-runtime with CodexKPprotoN_for_KPN for per-SNI policy, fake-TLS secret handling, bounded Telegram bootstrap, and private policy API behavior.
Steps.step-5: Restore M-050 RU SNI routing so issued wildcard SNI goes to DE KPprotoN runtime and web/admin HTTPS remains stable.
Steps.step-6: Update M-012 deploy wiring to start the KPprotoN DE runtime as the active data-plane and disable official MTProxy services in the production compose path.
Steps.step-7: Update M-045/M-047 user/admin surfaces only as needed for reissue messaging, redaction, and fake-TLS status semantics.
Steps.step-8: Strengthen M-051 diagnostics with Phase-41 redacted smoke for issued ee link, SNI route, policy apply without restart, DE runtime logs, and Telegram proof.
Steps.step-9: Run local verification, MyGRACE reviewer/refresh, then clean VPS deploy and operator Telegram Desktop/mobile acceptance before any release tag.

Verification.command-5: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py tests/test_kpproton_runtime_bridge.py tests/test_mtproto_api.py tests/test_mtproto_admin_ops.py tests/test_mtproto_de_edge_contract.py tests/test_mtproto_availability_diagnostics.py -v
Verification.command-7: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/plans/Phase-41.xml docs/modules/M-043.xml docs/modules/M-044.xml docs/modules/M-049.xml docs/modules/M-050.xml docs/modules/M-051.xml docs/modules/M-052.xml docs/modules/M-053.xml && mygrace lint
Verification.command-8: operator-live: clean VPS deploy, new verified user receives ee fake-TLS link, RU routes issued SNI to DE, DE policy_count changes without data-plane restart, and Telegram Desktop/mobile works.

VerificationMatrix.gate-1: Owner payload uses fake-TLS ee secret and issued user SNI, not official dd secret on the base domain.
VerificationMatrix.gate-2: Policy apply/revoke/replay updates KPprotoN runtime policy without restarting the MTProto data-plane for unchanged or newly added SNI policy.
VerificationMatrix.gate-3: mtproto-runtime matches the reference KPprotoN semantics for per-SNI fake-TLS policy and bootstrap fallback.
VerificationMatrix.gate-4: RU public 443 classifies web SNI and issued wildcard SNI correctly while preserving admin 8443 and private nginx fallback.
VerificationMatrix.gate-5: Official MTProxy runtime and manifest sync are not active in the production data-plane after Phase-41 execution.
VerificationMatrix.gate-6: Telegram Desktop and mobile connect through a newly issued Phase-41 proxy and remain connected beyond the initial availability check.

AcceptanceCriteria.item-1: New verified users receive a stable personal KPprotoN fake-TLS proxy with server under u-*.krotpn.xyz and secret prefix ee.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-2: Official dd-secret links are not issued as the production owner payload after Phase-41 execution.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-3: Adding a user or replaying assignments does not restart the MTProto data-plane for unchanged policy state.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-4: https://krotpn.xyz, https://krotpn.xyz:8443/login, backend API, registration email, and VPN provisioning remain healthy.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-5: Admin revoke/reissue affects exactly one user SNI policy and keeps all raw proxy secrets redacted from admin tables, audit events, logs, and smoke output.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-6: Telegram Desktop and phone both show the proxy available and traffic remains usable after initial connection, with evidence recorded as operator-live.

Risks.risk-1: Misreading the KPprotoN reference can reproduce the earlier fake-TLS mismatch; Phase-41 must compare KrotPN runtime behavior against CodexKPprotoN_for_KPN before live deploy.
Risks.risk-2: Changing public 443 routing can break web HTTPS or admin access if SNI classification or fallback ports drift.
Risks.risk-3: Wildcard TLS private key handling on DE remains sensitive and must stay redacted in deploy logs and diagnostics.
Risks.risk-4: Existing official-mode assignments need explicit reissue or migration messaging so users do not keep broken dd links.
Risks.risk-5: Local fake-TLS probes are not enough; final readiness depends on real Telegram Desktop/mobile evidence.

AutonomousExecutionSafety.safe-local: Planning docs, static smoke, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, backend tests with fake/private adapters, and Erlang compile from a safe ASCII temp path.
AutonomousExecutionSafety.requires-operator: Clean VPS deploy, public 443 cutover, wildcard certificate placement, RU/DE service restart, and Telegram Desktop/mobile proof.
AutonomousExecutionSafety.blocked-action: Secret rotation, wildcard certificate replacement, or broad deploy workflow redesign without a separate approved fix phase.
